name: Game Structure & UI
theme: State machine, title screen, pause menu, HUD, game over, and screen transitions

criteria.description: A game state machine exists managing at least these states: TITLE, STAGE, PAUSED, GAME_OVER, STAGE_COMPLETE. The current state is tracked and drives what is updated and rendered.
testSteps: Review the codebase for a state machine or game state tracking system
Verify at least 5 distinct states are defined: TITLE, STAGE, PAUSED, GAME_OVER, STAGE_COMPLETE
Verify the current state determines which update and render logic executes
Verify state transitions use a clean function/method (not ad-hoc flag toggling)
Verify each state has proper setup/initialization when entered
criteria.threshold: At least 5 game states with a central state variable driving update/render logic and clean transition functions

criteria.description: The game starts by showing a title screen with the game name 'Kingdoms of the Canvas' prominently displayed
testSteps: Open index.html in the browser
Wait 1 second for initialization
Take a screenshot of the canvas
Verify the title screen is displayed (not the gameplay stage)
Verify the text 'Kingdoms of the Canvas' (or a recognizable portion) is rendered on the canvas
Verify the player is NOT visible and gameplay is NOT active
criteria.threshold: Game opens to a title screen showing the game name; gameplay is not active

criteria.description: The title screen displays 'Press Enter to Start' text that is animated (pulsing, blinking, or fading)
testSteps: Open the game and view the title screen
Take a screenshot at time T
Take another screenshot at time T+500ms
Verify 'Press Enter to Start' or similar prompt text is visible
Verify the text appearance changes between screenshots (pulsing opacity, size, or color)
criteria.threshold: A 'Press Enter to Start' prompt is visible on the title screen with animated appearance

criteria.description: The title screen has an animated scrolling background — not a static solid color
testSteps: Open the game to the title screen
Take a screenshot at time T
Wait 2 seconds
Take a screenshot at time T+2000ms
Compare the two screenshots — verify the background has changed/moved (scrolling parallax, animated elements, or similar)
criteria.threshold: Title screen background is animated (scrolling, moving particles, or other visual motion)

criteria.description: Pressing Enter on the title screen transitions to gameplay (STAGE state) — the player appears and is controllable
testSteps: Open the game to the title screen
Press Enter
Wait 2 seconds for any transition animation to complete
Verify the game state has changed from TITLE to STAGE
Verify the player character is visible on screen
Verify the player responds to input (e.g., pressing ArrowRight moves the player)
criteria.threshold: Pressing Enter on the title screen starts gameplay with a visible, controllable player

criteria.description: The title screen has a Controls option — pressing C or selecting it shows a controls/bindings screen
testSteps: Open the game to the title screen
Verify a 'Controls' option or hint is visible on the title screen
Press the key to access controls (e.g., C)
Verify a controls screen appears showing keyboard bindings
Verify the controls screen lists at minimum: Arrow keys, Z (jump), X (attack), Shift (run), Escape (pause)
criteria.threshold: A controls screen is accessible from the title screen showing all key bindings

criteria.description: The controls screen can be dismissed to return to the title screen
testSteps: Navigate to the controls screen from the title screen
Press Escape or Enter (or a back button) to dismiss the controls screen
Verify the game returns to the title screen
Verify the title screen is fully functional again (Enter to start works)
criteria.threshold: Controls screen can be dismissed, returning to a fully functional title screen

criteria.description: Pressing Escape during gameplay (STAGE state) pauses the game and shows a pause menu overlay
testSteps: Start the game and enter the STAGE state
Press Escape
Verify a pause menu overlay appears on screen
Verify gameplay is frozen (player does not move, enemies/physics stop, timer pauses)
Take a screenshot confirming the pause menu is visible over the game
criteria.threshold: Pressing Escape during gameplay shows a visible pause menu and freezes game updates

criteria.description: The pause menu displays at least three options: Resume, Restart Stage, and Quit to Title
testSteps: Pause the game with Escape
Take a screenshot of the pause menu
Verify at least three menu items are visible: Resume (or Continue), Restart Stage (or Retry), and Quit to Title (or Return to Title)
Verify the menu items are selectable (one is highlighted/selected by default)
criteria.threshold: Pause menu shows at least 3 options including Resume, Restart, and Quit to Title

criteria.description: Selecting Resume (or pressing Escape again) closes the pause menu and resumes gameplay
testSteps: Pause the game with Escape
Verify the pause menu is visible
Select Resume or press Escape again
Verify the pause menu disappears
Verify gameplay resumes (player is controllable, physics active)
criteria.threshold: Resume option or Escape closes pause menu and returns to active gameplay

criteria.description: Selecting Restart Stage resets the current stage — player returns to spawn with full HP and the level resets
testSteps: Start gameplay and move the player away from spawn, take some damage
Pause the game and select Restart Stage
Verify the player returns to the level spawn position
Verify player health is restored to full (3 HP)
Verify any consumed breakable blocks or changed level state is reset
criteria.threshold: Restart Stage resets player position, health, and level state to initial values

criteria.description: Selecting Quit to Title returns the game to the title screen
testSteps: Start gameplay, then pause the game
Navigate to and select the Quit to Title option
Verify the game transitions to the title screen
Verify the title screen is fully displayed (game title, Press Enter prompt)
Verify pressing Enter starts a new game session
criteria.threshold: Quit to Title returns to a fully functional title screen

criteria.description: Pause menu options are navigable with arrow keys (Up/Down) and selectable with Enter
testSteps: Pause the game with Escape
Verify one menu option is highlighted by default
Press ArrowDown — verify the highlight moves to the next option
Press ArrowUp — verify the highlight moves back
Press Enter on a highlighted option — verify it activates
criteria.threshold: Menu options can be navigated with Up/Down arrows and activated with Enter

criteria.description: During gameplay, health hearts are displayed in the top-left corner — filled hearts for current HP, empty hearts for missing HP
testSteps: Start the game and enter gameplay
Take a screenshot — verify heart icons are visible in the top-left area
Verify 3 filled hearts are shown (player starts with 3 HP)
Cause the player to take damage (1 hit)
Take another screenshot — verify 2 filled hearts and 1 empty/outline heart
criteria.threshold: Health hearts in top-left show filled hearts for current HP and empty outlines for lost HP

criteria.description: During gameplay, a coin counter is displayed in the top-center area with a coin icon and count starting at 0
testSteps: Start the game and enter gameplay
Take a screenshot of the HUD area
Verify a coin icon (yellow circle, star, or similar) is visible in the top-center
Verify a numeric counter is displayed next to the coin icon
Verify the counter starts at 0 (or displays 'x0' / '0')
criteria.threshold: Coin counter with icon is visible in the top-center HUD, starting at 0

criteria.description: During gameplay, the current stage name is displayed in the top-right corner of the HUD
testSteps: Start the game and enter gameplay
Take a screenshot of the HUD area
Verify text is displayed in the top-right corner
Verify the text represents a stage name or identifier (e.g., 'Test Stage', 'World 1-1', or similar)
criteria.threshold: A stage name/identifier is visible in the top-right area of the HUD during gameplay

criteria.description: All HUD elements have semi-transparent dark backgrounds for readability against any game background
testSteps: Start the game and take a screenshot during gameplay
Inspect the HUD elements (hearts, coins, stage name) visually
Verify each HUD element group has a dark semi-transparent background panel behind it
Verify the text/icons are clearly readable against the backdrop
criteria.threshold: HUD elements sit on semi-transparent dark background panels, ensuring readability

criteria.description: When the player loses all lives, a Game Over screen is displayed with options to Continue or Quit
testSteps: Start the game and reduce player lives to 1 (via debug API or repeated deaths)
Kill the player to trigger the final death
Wait for the death animation and any transition to complete
Verify a Game Over screen/overlay appears
Verify at least two options are shown: Continue (restart) and Quit (return to title)
criteria.threshold: Game Over screen appears when lives reach 0, showing Continue and Quit options

criteria.description: Selecting Continue on the Game Over screen restarts the stage with refreshed lives and health
testSteps: Trigger the Game Over screen
Select the Continue option
Verify the game transitions back to the STAGE state
Verify the player is at the spawn point with full HP (3) and refreshed lives (5 or starting amount)
Verify the level is reset
criteria.threshold: Continue restarts the stage with full health and refreshed lives

criteria.description: Selecting Quit on the Game Over screen returns to the title screen
testSteps: Trigger the Game Over screen
Navigate to and select the Quit option
Verify the game transitions back to the TITLE state
Verify the title screen is displayed correctly
criteria.threshold: Quit returns to a fully functional title screen from the Game Over screen

criteria.description: A Stage Complete screen can be triggered (via debug API or reaching a level exit) showing completion info
testSteps: Start the game and enter gameplay
Trigger stage completion (via reaching an exit zone, debug API, or equivalent mechanism)
Verify a Stage Complete screen/overlay appears
Verify it displays at least: a congratulatory message, elapsed time, and coins collected
Verify an option to continue (return to title or proceed) is available
criteria.threshold: Stage Complete screen shows time, coins collected, and a way to proceed

criteria.description: Screen transitions between states use an iris-wipe effect — a circular mask that shrinks to black and expands on the new scene
testSteps: Start the game on the title screen
Press Enter to start gameplay
Take rapid screenshots during the transition (every 100ms for 1 second)
Verify at least one mid-transition frame shows a partially obscured screen (circular black mask)
Verify the transition is not an instant cut — there is a visible animated period
criteria.threshold: State transitions include a visible iris-wipe (circular mask) animation, not instant cuts

criteria.description: The iris-wipe transition completes fully — screen goes fully black at midpoint, then reveals the new scene
testSteps: Review the transition/iris-wipe implementation code
Verify the transition has two phases: closing (circle shrinks to zero) and opening (circle expands from zero)
Verify the state change happens at the midpoint (when screen is fully black)
Verify the transition has a configurable or fixed duration (approximately 0.5-1.5 seconds total)
Verify gameplay input is blocked during the transition
criteria.threshold: Iris-wipe has close and open phases with state change at the black midpoint; input blocked during transition

criteria.description: Game state is exposed via the debug API (window.__gameDebug) for testability — includes current state and state transition method
testSteps: Open the game
Check window.__gameDebug for a game state property (e.g., gameState, currentState, or state)
Verify the initial state is 'TITLE' or equivalent
Press Enter to start, wait for transition
Verify the debug state has changed to 'STAGE' or equivalent
criteria.threshold: Debug API exposes the current game state, reflecting actual state transitions

criteria.description: A boss health bar component exists in the HUD system — visible at bottom-center when a boss is active, hidden otherwise
testSteps: Review the HUD rendering code
Verify a boss health bar rendering function exists
Verify it renders at the bottom-center of the screen
Verify it only renders when a boss is active (conditional on boss state/data)
Verify it includes the boss name and a proportional health bar
criteria.threshold: Boss health bar code exists with conditional rendering, boss name label, and proportional fill

criteria.description: Menu and HUD systems are implemented in separate, modular files — not inlined in the game loop
testSteps: Review the project file structure
Verify HUD rendering is in a dedicated file or clearly separated module (e.g., hud.js)
Verify menu/state logic is in a dedicated file or module (e.g., states.js, menu.js)
Verify these modules are loaded in index.html
Verify the game loop delegates to these modules rather than containing their logic inline
criteria.threshold: HUD and menu/state logic are in dedicated modules, separate from the main game loop

criteria.description: All Sprint 1 & 2 player mechanics work within the STAGE state: movement (walk, run, jump, wall slide, wall jump, coyote time, crouch), combat (melee, charge, jump attack), health/lives, and all animations
testSteps: Start the game and enter gameplay (press Enter on title screen)
Verify the player falls under gravity and lands on solid tiles
Verify ArrowLeft/ArrowRight moves the player; Shift increases speed
Verify Z triggers a jump with variable height
Verify X triggers a melee attack with visible animation
Verify ArrowDown on ground triggers crouch with reduced hitbox
Verify the camera follows the player with parallax backgrounds
Verify hazard tiles deal damage and invincibility frames work
Verify the player has 3 HP and 5 lives
criteria.threshold: All Sprint 1 engine and Sprint 2 player mechanics remain fully functional in the STAGE state

criteria.description: Canvas renders at 960x540 logical pixels, game loop runs at ~60 FPS, and no JavaScript errors in the console
testSteps: Open index.html
Verify canvas.width === 960 and canvas.height === 540
Let the game run for 3 seconds
Verify the average FPS is >= 55
Check the browser console for any JavaScript errors
Verify no errors are present
criteria.threshold: 960x540 canvas, >= 55 FPS average, zero JS console errors

outOfScope: World Map screen and stage selection navigation — Sprint 4
Collectible (coin) pickup mechanics and spawning — Sprint 4
Enemy AI, enemy entities, and boss fights — Sprint 5
Boss health bar being testable with actual boss data — Sprint 5 (code structure only in Sprint 3)
Sound effects and music (menu select sounds, ambient audio) — Sprint 10
Advanced visual effects (screen shake, particle explosions beyond existing) — Sprint 11
Victory screen with scrolling credits and final stats — deferred until content exists (Sprint 9+)
Stage Complete triggered by a real level exit with door/goal — Sprint 5 (debug/API trigger for now)
Coin incrementing during gameplay (no coin pickups exist yet) — Sprint 4/5
Return to World Map option in pause menu — Sprint 4 (only Resume, Restart, Quit to Title for now)
notes: This sprint adds the game structure layer on top of the existing engine (Sprint 1) and player character (Sprint 2). The game now starts at a title screen instead of dropping directly into gameplay. A complete state machine manages transitions between Title, Stage (gameplay), Paused, Game Over, and Stage Complete states. The HUD overlays health hearts, a coin counter (starting at 0 — coins are not yet collectable until Sprint 4/5), and stage name during gameplay. A boss health bar component is implemented in code but only renders when boss data is present (Sprint 5). Iris-wipe transitions provide polished scene changes. Since the World Map doesn't exist until Sprint 4, the title screen transitions directly to the test stage. The Stage Complete state is triggerable via a debug API or simple exit zone for now — real level exits with doors will come with actual level content in Sprint 5+. The pause menu supports Resume, Restart Stage, and Quit to Title. Game Over provides Continue and Quit options.
